markets.summary: Three public listings reviewed in depth. Two adjacent Haste Street units publish similar prices but different finishes; one unit’s move-in block references a different studio number.
markets.source: Public listing pages and UC Berkeley Off-Campus Housing

markets.summary: The richest distortion sample: duplicate clusters, one property at two prices, four title/listed-price mismatches, and a stale sold listing.
Five studio results appeared in a $1,200–$3,000 Peninsula search; three of four priced examples were in East Palo Alto rather than Palo Alto.
The initial query was too restrictive to support a finding. No market conclusion is shown until the search is rerun.

patterns.label: Duplicate listing clusters
patterns.finding: Berkeley: ~10 posts at $2,845 · San Jose: ~10 at $2,397

patterns.label: Same property, two prices
patterns.finding: San Jose: Miranda Villa at $1,706 and $1,399

patterns.label: Headline price differs
patterns.finding: San Jose: 4 posts showed $1,850 or $2,115, then listed $2,088

patterns.label: Stale listing left published
patterns.finding: San Jose: a post marked “[SOLD]” remained live at $1,990
